rationale: The cited paper is a close conceptual predecessor: it studies debt management and shows how discrete subgoal completion (accounts settled) predicts long-run debt elimination, while the citing paper theorizes repayment-by-purchase via perceived progress and narrow-bracketed subgoals in credit-card repayment. Given this strong overlap in mechanism and domain (debt repayment/goal pursuit), plus an exact DOI match in the references, this is plausibly a substantive theory/empirical anchor rather than a generic mention.
